scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: All pass criteria met: 7 rules extracted with individual confidence/invariant ratings; 5+ escalations serving as clarifying questions; 'demand' explicitly identified as undefined (escalation #2); acceptance_criteria is an empty array (no Given/When/Then); overall confidence.level is 30 (<=40); no specific values invented as requirements.

observations.text: 7 well-reasoned business rules with invariant classifications and justifications — exceeds the 5-rule minimum
Demand explicitly called out as undefined in escalation #2 with concrete options for defining it
acceptance_criteria intentionally left empty — correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from vague input
confidence.level at 30 with thorough reasoning, clear separation of high vs low confidence areas, and explicit assumptions
5 detailed escalations with options and recommendations serve as clarifying questions covering demand definition, guardrails, price-locking, fallback behavior, and regulatory compliance
Edge cases thoughtfully identify gaps like demand feed failure, price flickering, and regulatory price-gouging — all marked as undefined
BR-6 (price > 0) and BR-7 (profitability) show the agent reasoning beyond the literal input to extract implied domain constraints
Escalations could have more explicitly surfaced 'pricing update frequency' as a standalone question — it is only indirectly addressed via the 'what is real-time' low-confidence note
Missing explicit question about whether dynamic pricing applies to all services or a subset, though this is a minor gap given the thoroughness elsewhere

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'ambiguity', got values: 'missing_context', 'missing_context', 'missing_context'
